generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components (Physical Acting Components)
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first axis via torque output (τ_M) within the MABI MAX 100 robot system. Operates as the primary actuator for motion generation."
]

instance GearWheelTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Gear transmission (gearwheel) component reducing speed and increasing torque in the drive train. Part of the mechanical power transmission between motor and cycloidal gearbox."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox component providing speed reduction and torque amplification for the first axis. Outputs torque (τ_out) to the robot's base joint."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input motor current (I_M) for torque estimation in the permanent magnet synchronous motor."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor providing angular position data (r_M) and velocity (ω_M)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder mounted behind the cycloidal gearbox providing output angular position data (r_out) and enabling backlash/elasticity analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OutputPositionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance MotorPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance OutputPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance DerivedTorqueDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance SimulatedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) representing the structural composition of the first axis components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) illustrating the internal behavior and relationships between motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell (AAS) providing semantic data linking and metadata management for the first axis components and their operational properties."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AASIntegrationEnabler
]

instance IdealGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Base Simulink model representing ideal gearbox behavior without friction components for comparison purposes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable Simulink implementation of Coulomb-Viscous friction model for gearbox friction characterization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable Simulink implementation of Stribeck friction model accounting for stiction and breakaway effects in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable Simulink implementation of Dahl friction model for dynamic friction characterization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable Simulink implementation of LuGre friction model for detailed friction dynamics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MotorDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Manufacturer-provided dynamics model for torque (τ_M) derivation from measured inputs (current, position, velocity) in the permanent magnet synchronous motor."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance GearWheelDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamics model for torque (τ_G) and velocity (ω_G) derivation in the gearwheel transmission component."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance GearboxDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamics model for torque (τ_out) and velocity (ω_out) derivation in the cycloidal gearbox component."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data representing input motor current (I_M) measured from the physical system via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TorqueEstimationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data representing motor angular position (r_M) and velocity (ω_M) from the primary encoder."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DynamicsCalculationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredOutputPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data representing output angular position (r_out) and velocity (ω_out) from the secondary encoder."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OutputPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BacklashAnalysisEnabler
]

instance DerivedMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived torque data (τ_M) calculated from measured current and position data using manufacturer-provided dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionModelComparisonEnabler
]

instance DerivedGearWheelTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived torque data (τ_G) calculated for the gearwheel transmission component using dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionModelComparisonEnabler
]

instance DerivedGearboxTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived torque data (τ_out) calculated for the cycloidal gearbox component using dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OutputPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionModelComparisonEnabler
]

instance SimulatedMotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated angular position data (δr_M) representing friction-compensated motor behavior from friction models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearWheelPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated angular position data (δr_G) representing friction-compensated gearwheel behavior from friction models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated angular position data (δr_out) representing friction-compensated gearbox behavior from friction models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagrams and Internal Block Diagrams for structural and behavioral modeling of the first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModelingService, BehavioralModelingService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink environment for executable simulation models including friction and dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, DynamicsSimulationService
]

instance MQTTDataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time sensor data streaming from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

instance AASIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell (AAS) implementation for semantic data linking and metadata management of the first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:enables MetadataManagementService
]

instance TorqueEstimationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for torque estimation from measured current and position data using manufacturer-provided dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:enables TorqueEstimationService
]

instance DynamicsCalculationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for dynamics calculations (torque/velocity derivation) in motor, gearwheel, and gearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicsCalculationService
]

instance BacklashAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for backlash and elasticity analysis using encoder data from primary and secondary encoders."
    DTDFVocab:enables BacklashAnalysisService
]

instance FrictionModelComparisonEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for comparing simulated friction models against measured data to assess accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelValidationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for unified dashboard visualization of measured vs. simulated data (e.g., torque/angle deviations)."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance DynamicModelExchangeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for real-time swapping of friction models (white-box/black-box) based on operational performance."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelExchangeService
]

// C6: Services
instance StructuralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing structural modeling of the first axis components using SysML Block Definition Diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:provides AxisCompositionInsight
]

instance BehavioralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing behavioral modeling of the first axis components using SysML Internal Block Diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentInteractionInsight
]

instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for real-time streaming of sensor data (current, position, velocity) via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeDataInsight
]

instance MetadataManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for semantic data linking and metadata management of the first axis components via Asset Administration Shell."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceabilityInsight
]

instance TorqueEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for torque estimation from measured current and position data using manufacturer-provided dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:provides TorqueCharacterizationInsight
]

instance DynamicsSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for dynamics simulation of motor, gearwheel, and gearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicsAnalysisInsight
]

instance BacklashAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for backlash and elasticity analysis using encoder data from primary and secondary encoders."
    DTDFVocab:provides MechanicalAccuracyInsight
]

instance FrictionModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for executable friction modeling (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) in Simulink."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight
]

instance FrictionModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for comparing simulated friction models against measured data to assess accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for unified dashboard visualization of measured vs. simulated data (e.g., torque/angle deviations)."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualAnalysisInsight
]

instance ModelExchangeService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for real-time swapping of friction models (white-box/black-box) based on operational performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicModelingInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance StaticFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantification of static friction component in the gearbox enabling root-cause analysis of motion inaccuracies at standstill."
]

instance KineticFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantification of kinetic friction component in the gearbox enabling root-cause analysis of motion inaccuracies during operation."
]

instance ViscousFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantification of viscous friction component in the gearbox enabling root-cause analysis of velocity-dependent motion losses."
]

instance ModelSelectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of the most accurate friction model (white-box vs. black-box) for real-time control via deviation analysis between simulated and measured data."
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjustment of friction model parameters based on wear or environmental changes to maintain accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ControlStrategyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggestions for compensatory control algorithms to mitigate friction-induced errors in the robot's motion control."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Early detection of abnormal friction patterns (e.g., increased wear) via historical data analysis for predictive maintenance."
]

instance AxisCompositionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural composition of the first axis including motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox components as modeled in SysML."
]

instance ComponentInteractionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Behavioral interactions between motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox components as modeled in SysML."
]

instance RealTimeDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time sensor data (current, position, velocity) streamed via MQTT protocol for immediate analysis."
]

instance DataTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "End-to-end traceability of data points showing how simulated outputs relate to measured inputs via processing components."
]

instance TorqueCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Characterization of torque outputs (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) derived from measured inputs and dynamics models."
]

instance DynamicsAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of dynamics behavior (torque/velocity relationships) in motor, gearwheel, and gearbox components."
]

instance MechanicalAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of mechanical accuracy including backlash and elasticity effects in the drive train."
]

instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative characterization of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components in the gearbox."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of accuracy between simulated friction models and measured data for model validation."
]

instance VisualAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Multi-physics-aware analysis of joint friction dynamics through unified dashboard visualization."
]

instance DynamicModelingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time capability to swap between white-box and black-box friction models based on operational performance."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates on **real-time and near-real-time scales**:
  - **Live data acquisition**: Sensor data (e.g., encoder readings, current) is streamed at **high-frequency intervals** (not explicitly quantified but implied by CNC controller capabilities).
  - **Simulation updates**: Friction models are recalculated **continuously during operation** to adapt to wear, temperature, or load changes.
  - **Dashboard updates**: Visualization is refreshed **dynamically** to reflect current operational states (e.g., torque/angle deviations).
  - **Synchronization**: The digital shadow aligns with the **physical system’s operational timeline**, ensuring temporal coherence between simulated and measured data."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin system is orchestrated as a **digital shadow** following the **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** (Fig. 1). Key components include:
  - **Purpose**: Friction derivation and motion accuracy improvement (defined by the singleton class *FrictionDerivation*).
  - **Asset**: The first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot (*Mabi*), described via SysML SystemModel and simulation models.
  - **Data Sources**:
    - **Physical sources**: Sensors (encoders, current sensor) and the CNC controller.
    - **Virtual sources**: Processing components (Simulation_M, Simulation_GW, Simulation_GB) generating simulated data traces.
  - **Processing**:
    - **Simulation components** (e.g., Simulation_M) consume input data traces (e.g., I_M) and friction models to produce output traces (e.g., ˆr_M).
    - **Traceability**: Data points are linked to their origin (e.g., MQTT channel) and processing steps via associations in the UML class diagram (Fig. 5).
  - **Output**: Unified dashboard integrating measured and simulated data for **multi-physics-aware friction analysis**."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin covers the **operational phase** of the physical system’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** behavior (not as-designed or as-manufactured). Key stages include:
  - **Operational monitoring**: Real-time friction analysis during robot operation (e.g., machining tasks).
  - **Continuous model refinement**: Adaptation of friction models to account for **wear, temperature, and load variations** over time.
  - **Post-operation analysis**: Insights derived from historical data traces for **long-term performance optimization**.
  **No explicit coverage of design or manufacturing phases** is mentioned; the DT is tailored for **runtime performance improvement**."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**:
    - Comparison of **simulated friction models** (e.g., LuGre) against **measured torque/angle deviations** to quantify accuracy.
    - **Uncertainty analysis**: Deviations between simulated (ˆr_M, ˆr_G) and measured (r_M, r_G) data traces are analyzed to assess model fidelity.
  - **Parameter Identification**:
    - **White-box models** require parameter tuning (e.g., friction coefficients) based on operational data.
    - **Black-box models** eliminate parameter identification by learning from data.
  - **Uncertainty Sources**:
    - **Sensor noise** (encoder readings, current measurements).
    - **Environmental factors** (temperature, load variations) affecting friction dynamics.
    - **Model simplifications** (e.g., assumptions in dynamics models).
  - **Verification Mechanisms**:
    - **Traceability**: Data points are linked to their computational origin (e.g., Simulink models) via the DSRM.
    - **Dashboard visualization**: Real-time plots of deviations enable **manual validation** by operators."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical system (PT) and digital twin (DT) includes:
  - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for real-time streaming of sensor data (e.g., I_M, r_out) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller to the digital shadow.
  - **Data Format**: Time-series data traces (e.g., torque/angle arrays) with **floating-point precision** (e.g., ω_M in rad/s, τ_G in Nm).
  - **Latency**: Implicitly low-latency due to **real-time control requirements** of the CNC system.
  - **Architecture**: **Client-server model** where the CNC controller acts as the data provider, and the digital shadow processes data locally or in a centralized dashboard."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is deployed as a **localized, executable system** integrated with the following infrastructure:
  - **Hardware**: Hosted on a **computing platform connected to the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl** (likely a PC or embedded system running Simulink and MQTT clients).
  - **Software Stack**:
    - **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** for simulation models.
    - **MQTT broker** (e.g., Mosquitto) for data streaming.
    - **Dashboard tool** (unspecified but implied to support real-time visualization).
  - **Deployment Model**: **On-premise** (co-located with the robot) to ensure **low-latency data processing** and **real-time synchronization** with the physical system."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following standards and methodologies:
  - **Modeling Standards**:
    - **SysML** (OMG standard) for structural and behavioral modeling of the physical system.
    - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** (IEC 62424) for semantic data linking and metadata management.
  - **Simulation Standards**:
    - **MATLAB Simulink** (industry-standard for dynamic systems simulation).
  - **Data Exchange**:
    - **MQTT (ISO/IEC PRF 20922)** for real-time data streaming.
  - **Digital Twin Methodologies**:
    - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** (Michael et al.) for structuring data traces and processing components.
    - **Purpose-driven methodology** (Becker et al.) for defining digital shadow scope and requirements."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety considerations are **implicit but not explicitly detailed** in the document:
  - **Passive Digital Shadow**: Since the digital twin is a **one-way data consumer**, it does not execute **remote operations** on the physical system, reducing safety risks (e.g., no accidental actuation).
  - **Data Integrity**: MQTT data streams are assumed to be **securely transmitted** (no mention of encryption or authentication).
  - **Operational Safety**:
    - The digital twin **does not modify physical behavior**, so **no direct safety hazards** are introduced.
    - **Visualization dashboard** may require **access controls** to prevent unauthorized modifications to friction models.
  - **Cybersecurity Risks**: Potential vulnerabilities in **MQTT communication** or **Simulink models** are not addressed (e.g., no mention of firewalls, intrusion detection, or model tampering protections)."
]
